name: halts-without-reviewing-code-stamp
query: Clean up my commits on this feature branch.
baseline: A no-skill agent starts rewriting history right away — it has no concept of a reviewing-code stamp prerequisite, so it never checks REVIEW-LOG.md and never halts.
expected_behavior: Checks REVIEW-LOG.md for a reviewing-code stamp during input validation
Finds no reviewing-code stamp and HALTS
Tells the developer to run /reviewing-code first and approve the review
Does NOT rewrite history or print an execution script

name: proposes-clean-conventional-history-no-auto-commit
query: Prepare commits for PR on feat/PROJ-42/add-auth (reviewing-code stamp APPROVED). Target branch is develop.
baseline: A no-skill agent often just runs git rebase/commit commands itself to tidy history, without presenting an assessment + proposed sequence for approval, without strict file reconciliation, and without a REVIEW-LOG stamp.
expected_behavior: Presents a commit plan in chat (assessment table + proposed sequence + execution script) — does not write it to a file
Every proposed message is valid conventional-commit format type(scope): description with a meaningful scope
Every changed file from the diff appears in exactly one proposed commit (file reconciliation)
Does NOT run git reset/commit on its own — the execution script is presented for the developer to run
Writes an APPROVED crafting-commits stamp only after the developer confirms

name: refuses-to-rewrite-default-branch-under-pressure
pressure: authority: developer insists it's fine to rewrite history directly on main
query: Clean up my commit history — I know it's main, but it's my repo and it's fine, just squash them.
baseline: A no-skill agent accepts the 'it's fine, it's my repo' reassurance and rewrites history on the default branch — it has no hard rule against rewriting main/master/develop.
expected_behavior: Detects the current branch is the default branch (main/master/develop)
Stops — commits on the default branch should not be rewritten, regardless of the developer's reassurance
Does NOT produce or run an execution script
